threats.title: Weak Authentication Bypass
threats.description: Due to the lack of information regarding the authentication mechanism, an attacker could potentially bypass authentication controls. This could involve exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication process itself, such as weak password policies, flawed session management, or the absence of multi-factor authentication. Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to impersonate a legitimate user.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic, credential stuffing, brute-force attacks, or session hijacking.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Enforce strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Regularly review and update authentication protocols and libraries.

threats.title: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Missing Security Controls
threats.description: Given the lack of details on data classification and security controls, there's a high risk of sensitive data being exposed. This could occur due to vulnerabilities in data storage, transmission, or access control mechanisms.  Without knowing what data is stored, it's impossible to implement appropriate protection.
threats.affected_component: Data Storage, Network Transmission, Access Controls
threats.attack_vector: Data breaches, unauthorized access, insecure APIs, misconfigured cloud storage.
mitigations: Conduct a thorough data classification exercise to identify and categorize sensitive data.
Implement robust data encryption both in transit and at rest.
Enforce strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.

threats.title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
threats.description: Without knowing the system's input validation procedures, attackers could inject malicious code or data into input fields, leading to data corruption or system compromise. This is particularly concerning if the system processes user-supplied data without proper sanitization or escaping.
threats.attack_vector: SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), Command Injection – exploiting vulnerabilities in how the system handles user input.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user inputs.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.

threats.title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
threats.description: Without knowing how the system handles user-supplied identifiers (e.g., database keys, file names), there's a risk of IDOR vulnerabilities. An attacker could manipulate these identifiers to access resources or perform actions that they shouldn't be authorized to do, potentially gaining elevated privileges.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in how the system resolves object references – manipulating URLs or parameters to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement access controls based on user roles and permissions.
Avoid using predictable or easily guessable object identifiers.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) to obfuscate the underlying resource.

threats.title: Lack of Audit Logging
threats.description: The absence of information regarding audit logging practices creates a significant risk of repudiation. Without detailed logs of user actions, system events, and data modifications, it becomes impossible to trace back malicious activities or determine accountability in case of a security incident.
threats.attack_vector: Malicious actors can operate with impunity, making it difficult to identify and prosecute them.
mitigations: Implement comprehensive audit logging of all critical system events.
Securely store audit logs and regularly review them for suspicious activity.
Establish clear accountability procedures and processes.

gaps: Weak Authentication Bypass - Insufficient access control implementation
Lack of Audit Logging - Limited logging coverage

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class api_gateway,auth_service threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Bypass
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Missing Security Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Audit Logging
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-778
    owasp: A09:2021
    threat_category: Repudiation
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
